mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL year-over-year increase above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields a negative information coefficient over 20-day horizons in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days exclusively during periods of high market volatility.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when VIX exceeds 20, or the signal shows no predictive power in high-volatility regimes.
